title: Forward Propagation: Data Flowing Through a Network
concept: Forward propagation is the process where input data flows sequentially forward through each layer of a neural network to produce a final prediction.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the main benefit of AdamW over plain Adam?

options.text: It decouples weight decay (regularization) from the main gradient update for better generalization.
It runs in parallel across multiple GPUs automatically.
It deletes all negative activation values.

exercises.explain: Correct! AdamW improves on Adam by separating weight decay from the gradient steps.

cards.title: The downstream river
cards.body: If you drop a leaf in a fast-flowing river, it travels downstream. It passes the first bend, floats under a bridge, and eventually reaches the ocean. It only ever moves in one direction — forward. Data in a neural network travels the same way during a prediction.

cards.title: Forward Propagation
cards.body: Forward propagation (or forward pass) is the process of feeding input data (e.g. an image) into a neural network and running calculations layer-by-layer to produce a prediction at the output layer.

cards.title: The factory assembly line
cards.body: Think of forward propagation like a factory assembly line. Raw materials (input layer) enter. Station 1 adds parts (first hidden layer). Station 2 paints it (second hidden layer). The final station packs it (output layer). Each station performs its specific task and passes the product forward until the final car rolls out.

cards.title: Classifying a handwritten digit
cards.body: An image of a handwritten '7' enters. The **Input Layer** reads the pixel values. The **Hidden Layer** combines these pixels to detect lines and corners. The **Output Layer** outputs the final probability score for the digit 7. The data moved sequentially forward at every step.

cards.title: Forward pass doesn't adjust weights
cards.body: Beginners often think the model learns and adjusts its weights during the forward pass. In reality, during forward propagation, all weights are **frozen**. The network is only using its current rules to make a guess; the learning and adjustment happen later in the backward pass.

cards.title: Forward Flow
cards.body: How data moves through the network layers:

left.text:
Raw features enter.
Multiplied by weights.
Activation function applied.

right.text:
Processed features passed forward.
Final output calculated.
Prediction returned.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary direction of data flow during forward propagation?

options.text: Sequentially forward from the input layer, through hidden layers, to the output layer.
Backward from the output layer to the input layer to adjust weights.
Randomly jumping between layers to maximize entropy.
Only within the hidden layers without ever reaching the output.

exercises.explain: Correct! Forward propagation flows strictly forward from input to output.

exercises.prompt: True or false: A neural network updates and corrects its weight parameters during the forward propagation phase.
exercises.explain: False! Weights are frozen during the forward pass; updates only happen during the backward pass.

exercises.sentence: Forward propagation is the process used by a neural network to make a ___.
exercises.explain: The goal of the forward pass is to compute a prediction for a given input.

options.text: A trained model accepts an email's words, calculates layer values, and outputs a 90% probability that it is spam.
A model compares its prediction to the correct label and adjusts its weights.
A developer writes a python script to collect raw training data.

exercises.explain: Correct! Computing layer values from input to output to get a prediction is forward propagation.

exercises.prompt: You are running a neural network in production to classify images. A user uploads a new image. What phase of the model is triggered, and are the weights updated?

options.text: Forward propagation is triggered to compute the classification; the weights remain completely frozen.
Backward propagation is triggered; the weights are adjusted based on the user's feedback.
Both forward and backward passes run, updating the weights for the next user.
The activation functions are disabled to speed up the server response.

exercises.explain: Exactly! When making live predictions (inference), we only run the forward pass to get the answer, and the model's weights remain frozen. Next, let's look at how we calculate these layer transformations at scale using matrix math!
